title: Fake Crypto Trading Platforms Targeting Share Trading Group Members. Scammers recruit via messaging app groups promoting stock tips, offering exclusive trading platforms claiming exceptional returns. Victims lose access to funds after deposit. High volume of new platform variations emerging weekly.

Surge in Qantas Impersonation Scams via SMS/Email. Attackers pose as Qantas using airline logos, claiming expiring points, unclaimed refunds, or gift redemptions. Links direct to credential-harvesting phishing sites. Originally identified August 2025, now spiking with account security variant.

ACCC Phone Number Spoofing for Personal Data Extraction. Scammers spoof ACCC contact numbers, claiming to investigate fraud or offer refunds. Requests for passwords, bank details, and security information follow urgency narrative. Publicised phone numbers from ACCC website used for social engineering.

Scambling Epidemic: Fake Online Casinos Operating Across Australia. Telstra blocked 1,800+ domains January-May 2026. Criminals replicate legitimate casino branding, offer bonuses, present fake lobbies indistinguishable from real operators. Financial and personal data harvesting primary goal.

Fraudulent ASIC Letters Claiming Bank Staff Investigation. Counterfeit ASIC-branded letters state bank employees under investigation, instruct fund transfers to secure accounts. Letters mimic official formatting and branding. Designed to exploit trust in financial regulator authority.

PALM Scheme Workers Targeted for Loan Scams. Seasonal migrant workers offered personal loan assistance via acquaintances. Scammers request upfront fees, gain banking access, then transfer approved loan funds to fraudster accounts. Known victims from co-workers and community members.

Well-Known Company Impersonation via SMS for Credential Theft. Scammers impersonate Amazon, YouTube and other major brands via text, requesting account verification or payment updates. Links direct to spoofed login pages capturing credentials. Daily volume affecting diverse consumer base.

Fake Australia Post Delivery Notifications via SMS/Email. Scammers send urgent messages claiming parcel requires fee or address update. Links harvest payment details and personal information. Volume peaks around peak shopping periods exploiting expected deliveries.

Microsoft Teams Guest Invitation Billing Alert Scam. Criminals send Teams invitations mimicking billing alerts (subscription charges, payment issues). Notifications originate from legitimate Microsoft addresses, bypass email filters. Phone numbers or payment instructions embedded in group names.

AFP Impersonation for Cryptocurrency Fund Theft. Scammers pose as AFP officers investigating data breaches or financial crimes, demanding urgent cryptocurrency transfers or seed phrase disclosure. Pressure tactics and false arrest threats used to expedite victim compliance.

Medicare Impersonation for Personal/Financial Data. Scammers send messages with links to fake Medicare websites claiming account restoration or card replacement. Requests for personal details, banking information, and payment to restore access. Exploits healthcare access anxiety.

Investment Scam Losses Hit $837.7M Annually. Fake investment platforms via messaging groups and social media targeting retail investors. Promise exceptional returns through AI trading or cryptocurrency schemes. 2025 saw $2.18B total scam losses across all types, investment category dominant.
